sections.paragraph:
κερματιστής, οῦ, ὁ (s. κέρμα; exc. for PGissUniv 30, 14 [III/IV a.d.], cited in L-S-J-M Suppl., not found outside our lit., but κερματίζω is used freq. in papyri in the sense ‘change money’; the rdg. κερμ. [for χρηματιστής] in Maximus Tyr. 31, 2b and d has no support in the ms. tradition) money-changer. In the outer court of the temple (s. Jos., Ant. 15, 410–17) J 2:14.—On coinage in use s. Schürer II 66f; and esp. 272 n. 54 (lit.).—DELG s.v. κείρω. M-M. Spicq.

exc. exc. = except

PGissUniv PGissUniv = Mitteilungen aus der Papyrussammlung der Giessener Universitätsbibliothek—List 4

L-S-J-M L-S-J-M = (L-S-J) HLiddell/RScott, A Greek-English Lexicon; New Edition by HSJones/RMcKenzie—List 6

Suppl.

Suppl. = Supplement (to serials)

suppl. = supplement, supplied (in grammatical references)

lit. lit. = literal(ly); literature (refererences to [scholarly] literature)

freq. freq. = frequent(ly)

rdg. rdg. = reading(s)

Maximus Tyr Maximus Tyr , II a.d.—List 5

  1. ms. = manuscript(s)

Jos. Jos. = Josephus. This abbr. used when follow by title; I a.d.—Lists 5

esp. esp. = especially

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

M-M M-M = JMoulton/GMilligan, Vocabulary of Greek Testament—Lists 4, 6

Spicq Spicq = CS., Lexique théologique du Nouveau Testament—Lists 6

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 541
